vitals.terrain: rolling hills with vineyards and lakeside
vitals.participation_trend: growing, sold out in past years

course_description.overview: Scenic 160 km route through Okanagan wine country, alongside Okanagan Lake, Skaha Lake, and vineyards, starting and finishing in Penticton.[1]

key_segments.name: King of the Mountain climb
key_segments.description: Signature climb with KOM/QOM competitions, fastest times around 9-12 minutes.

key_segments.name: Lakeside sections
key_segments.description: Rolling roads along Okanagan and Skaha Lakes with vineyard views.

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Challenging gran fondo with significant elevation, rolling terrain, and a key mountain climb in beautiful wine country setting.[4]

logistics.getting_there: Fly into Penticton Regional Airport or Kelowna International (1 hour drive); event in Skaha Lake Park expo area.[4]
logistics.accommodation: Hotels and resorts in Penticton and Okanagan wine country; book early due to festival weekend.
logistics.registration_details: Registers early, sells out; multiple distances available including gran fondo (160 km).[4]
logistics.bike_logistics: Start/finish in Penticton; road bikes recommended for paved routes.
logistics.local_considerations: Summer weather with Okanagan sunshine; family-friendly weekend with expo, kids' events, and wine region activities.[6]

final_verdict.one_liner: Premier Canadian gran fondo in stunning wine country led by cycling legend Axel Merckx.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Road cyclists seeking scenic challenge, wine lovers, and families for the full weekend experience.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for the beauty, organization, and community in BC's top gran fondo.
pro_tips: Register early as it sells out.
Bring family for kids' piccolofondo and expo.

youtube_data.search_query: Axel Merckx Gran Fondo gran fondo cycling Penticton

videos.title: Ride 181 - 10th Annual Okanagan Gran Fondo (2022)
videos.description:
This is the 10th annual Okanagan Granfondo that took place on Sunday, July 10, 2022. Starting in Penticton, B.C., the 3,000 participants rode the 153 km course north along the westside of Okanagan Lake to Summerland for the KOM climb before returning back to Penticton and then south along the eastside of Skaha Lake to Okanagan Falls. From there, riders headed south through Oliver, past the Burrowing Owl vineyard on the northside of Osoyoos before joining Highway 97 for the ride back north to Penticton. 

If interested, please support me in the Tour de Cure to benefit the BC Cancer Foundation  https://tourdecure.ca/participant/2884131/4014

At Fairview Gold Course, we headed west into the hills to ride through the White Lake Grasslands Protected Area. It is an absolutely spectacular cycling area and I loved it. After that, we rejoined Hwy 97 for the final descent into Penticton.

The ride started at 6:30 am with a beautiful cool temperature. The sun eventually took over and temperatures got up to the mid to high 20's.
videos.curation_reason: Recent full-length ride-along video with good viewership covering the complete 153km course experience.

videos.title: PGAMO Video Teaser
videos.description: http://www.granfondoaxelmerckx.com - Join us on Sunday, July 12th, 2020, for the 10th Prospera Granfondo Axel Merckx Okanagan (PGAMO) in Penticton, British Columbia, Canada.

terrain_notes.text: Race goes all along the vineyards with spectacular views while reaching 1600 meters elevation gain.

additional_quotes.text: Last year I had a pretty bad crash so it's a bit of a relief to be able to get to the end without losing skin and damaging my bike.
With riders finishing the race you can bet you'll find them sitting on patios enjoying a cold one this afternoon.

rider_intel.search_text: The Axel Merckx Gran Fondo in Penticton starts at 7 AM from Lake Shore Drive and Maid Street in downtown Penticton. The race offers three different distances, with the 160 km being the main event featuring 1600 meters of elevation gain. The course travels through vineyard terrain with spectacular views throughout. The race attracts riders of various ages, including younger participants in the 55 km Corto Fondo distance. Crashes can be a concern as noted by riders, but the post-race atmosphere is social with finishers gathering on patios for refreshments. The event combines challenging elevation with scenic vineyard landscapes characteristic of the British Columbia wine region.
